source.definition: a respectful term for a woman, often implying nobility or politeness

explanation.sourceText: The English word "lady" originates from Old English "hlǣfdige", which referred to a woman of high status or the female head of a household. Over time, it evolved to mean a polite or respectful term for any woman.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „lady“ provine din engleza veche „hlǣfdige“, care desemna o femeie de rang înalt sau capul feminin al unei gospodării. În timp, a evoluat pentru a desemna un termen politicos sau respectuos pentru orice femeie.

source.additional: In modern usage, "lady" can be used both formally and informally to refer to women with respect.

target.definition: a preda sau a livra ceva; femeie respectabilă

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian word "dame" derives from the French "dame" and denotes both a respectable woman and the playing card "queen".
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul românesc „dame“ provine din francezul „dame“ și desemnează atât o femeie respectabilă, cât și figura de joc „damă“.

target.additional: Das Wort „dame“ wird oft für Frauen in verschiedenen sozialen Kontexten verwendet.

examples.sourceText: The lady wore a beautiful dress.
The lady at the reception is very friendly.
examples.targetText: Dama de la recepție este foarte prietenoasă.

learningTips.text: Think of a "lady" as a polite and respectful term for a woman, often used in formal settings.
Remember the word "dame" in English, which also means a woman of rank or a title of honor, similar to the Romanian "dame".
The French origin of "dame" helps connect it to the English "lady" as both imply respect and nobility.
Picture a lady handing over something politely to link the meaning of "dame" with giving or delivering.
